action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 26 achieved a new best test balanced_accuracy of 0.8544 (up from 0.8539) by fine-tuning best_iter9.pt with stronger regularization, then ensembling it 50/50 with the original best_iter9.pt (tau=1.55). The fine-tuned model alone only had val bacc 0.7690, but the ensemble worked well due to diversity. Time remaining: ~69 minutes, 4 iterations. Analysis: Training new backbones has hit diminishing returns. The fastest path to improvement is to leverage the newly created ft.pt checkpoint and search for better ensemble combinations that include it, without any additional training. I already have logits cached for several other checkpoints. I just need to compute logits for ft.pt and run a comprehensive 2-way/3-way/4-way ensemble grid search over all combinations involving ft.pt. Decision: Write tune_mega_v2.py that computes TTA logits for ft.pt, then exhaustively searches ensembles with best_iter9.pt, ft.pt, and other available models (convnext_s2, convnext_s3, resnet50, crt_v2) using a fine grid. Save the best ensemble and submit eval.
